fix(resilience): gracefully handle read-only /data filesystem

writable, which broke every tool wrapped in withResilience
(all analytics + discovery tools). Health persistence is now
best-effort — tools continue working even without state file.

Add admin dashboard with usage tracking and spend limits

- Pricing: claude-opus-4-6 at $15/MTok input, $75/MTok output;
  Google CSE first 100 queries/day free then $5/1000
- DB tables created via existing inline migration pattern on startup

commit.message:
fix: auto-build Other Banks from client competitor config

Previously hardcoded with client 1776 local banks (Mechanics Bank,
SESLOC, SLO CU, etc.) that were irrelevant for other clients.

Now dynamically builds from COMPETITOR_MERCHANTS using all competitor
starts_with patterns + payment suffixes (PAYMENT, TRANSFER, ONLINE
PMT, MOBILE PMT, CHECK PYMT). Automatically adapts when CLIENT_ID
changes -- Cape Cod banks for 1615, CA banks for 1776, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Claude autofix workflow (Phase 2)

Triggered by commenting "@claude fix" on any PR. Claude reads the
diff, generates a patch, and pushes the fix directly to the branch.
Falls back to posting the suggested diff as a comment if the patch
can't be applied cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add AuthorityOps domain — reasoning-bound authority enforcement

AuthorityOps is the 4th domain mode in Deep Sigma, binding authority,
action, rationale, expiry, and audit into a single evaluable control
plane. Sits at: IntelOps → ReOps → AuthorityOps → Execution → FranOps.

Core:
- 11-step policy evaluation pipeline with 6 verdict statuses
  (ALLOW, BLOCK, ESCALATE, EXPIRED, MISSING_REASONING, KILL_SWITCH_ACTIVE)
- 12 function handlers (AUTH-F01..F12) following DomainMode pattern
- 7 authority modules: policy_runtime, policy_compiler, authority_graph,
  delegation_chain, reasoning_gate, decision_authority_resolver,
  authority_audit (hash-chained)
- Package restructure: authority.py → authority/ package (backward compat)

Integration:
- Memory Graph: +3 NodeKind, +8 EdgeKind, +3 ingest methods
- Cascade: +6 rules (CASCADE-R08..R13) connecting AuthorityOps
- FEEDS: +12 functions, +12 events in routing table
- Enterprise: 5 Protocol interfaces (identity, policy, approval, audit, kill-switch)

Tests: 746 passed (65 new AuthorityOps + 681 existing unchanged)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: DeepSigma
repository.description: DeepSigma turns AI decisions into auditable, recoverable infrastructure. It seals each decision episode end-to-end (inputs, actions, outcomes), detects drift, enforces governance gates, and drives patch loops with measurable confidence. Built in Python with MCP, LangChain, and OpenTelemetry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add immutable Transaction ledger — one row per payment event

Problem: payments accumulated into a single EventRegistration row, so
additional charges (e.g. admin-added dancer) silently overwrote the
original amount and date rather than appearing as a separate line.

Solution:
- New Transaction model/table (registration_id, event_id, user_id,
  amount, payment_status, stripe_session_id, description,
  student_count, observer_count, created_at)
- _record_transaction() helper deduplicates by stripe_session_id so
  webhook + verify-payment racing on the same session cannot create two
  rows for the same charge
- Free registrations, Stripe payments (verify-payment + webhook), and
  admin-finalize all call _record_transaction() to insert a new row
- Stripe session metadata now includes student_count/observer_count
  so the webhook can build an accurate description without a DB lookup
- GET /events/my-payments and GET /admin/payments query Transaction
  first (one row per payment); fall back to EventRegistration rows
  that pre-date the new table so existing history is preserved
- Frontend: renamed "Dancers" column to "Details" and shows the
  per-transaction description (e.g. "3 dancer(s)", "Added 1 dancer");
  added admin-paid badge styling (purple) in both dashboard and admin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix streaming: use http.client instead of urllib for true chunk streaming

socket access for real-time chunk forwarding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stale GCE encoding cache causing missing video files (#499)

## Summary
- When admin deletes outputs and re-triggers the video worker, the GCE
encoding worker returns a stale cached result (409/complete) but the
actual files no longer exist in GCS
- This caused YouTube upload to silently skip and the job to complete in
a half-published state (Dropbox has stems but no video, no YouTube
upload)
- Detected on job `f4a552bf` (Queen Anne - Let's Dance)

## Changes
- **Stale cache detection**: `_download_gce_encoded_files` now raises
`RuntimeError` when zero files are downloaded from GCS
- **Auto-retry with new job ID**: `_run_encoding` catches the stale
cache error and re-submits encoding with a suffixed job ID
(`{job_id}_retry_{uuid}`) so the GCE worker treats it as a fresh job
- **Distribution warning**: `_upload_to_youtube` now adds to
`distribution_warnings` when no video file is available (previously
silently returned)
- 3 new tests covering stale cache detection, retry logic, and YouTube
missing-file warning

## Test plan
- [x] All 57 orchestrator tests pass (54 existing + 3 new)
- [ ] Deploy and re-trigger job `f4a552bf` to verify fix in production

@coderabbitai ignore

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: increase worker memory to 1024MB + fix Terraform workflow

- Worker OOM-killed during BLS data refresh at 512MB — increase to 1024MB

commit.message:
Implement Phase 5: Project Browser + Sync Core

- Add Prisma models: Project, ProjectMember, ProjectArtifact,
  SyncOperation, SyncSnapshot with migration SQL
- Implement project CRUD API (create with artifact bootstrap,
  list with search/sort/filter, get, update, archive)
- Add membership endpoints (add/remove members with OWNER checks)
- Build artifact storage API (list, get, upsert with revision tracking)
- Implement sync queue system: batch push with revision validation,
  pull since revision, conflict resolution (keep-local/keep-remote/merged),
  force push/pull for owner recovery
- Add auto-merge for append-only artifacts (ideas, chat logs)
  and snapshot creation before merges
- Create client-side SyncQueue class (enqueue, flush, pull, getStatus)
- Build project dashboard UI with grid/list view, search, sort, filter,
  create project form, status badges
- Build project workspace UI with file tree, project overview,
  sync status indicator, sub-view navigation
- Build conflict resolver UI with side-by-side diff, resolution actions
- Add desktop sync IPC handlers (getProjects, pushOperations,
  pullChanges, getStatus) and update preload
- Redirect root page to /dashboard
- Add 35 new tests (100 total): project helpers, merge logic,
  snapshot creation, sync queue client
- Capture validation screenshots (desktop + mobile) via Playwright

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hash password with SHA-256 to hide from source inspection

Password is now stored as a SHA-256 hash instead of plaintext.
User input is hashed via Web Crypto API and compared against the
stored hash, so viewing source no longer reveals the password.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🔧 本番対応：OAuth認証・冪等性・部分成功対応

指摘された問題点を修正し、本番運用可能な設計に改善。

主な変更:
1. YouTube OAuth 2.0認証のCI対応
   - init_youtube_auth.py: ローカル初回認証専用
   - youtube_uploader.py: run_local_server()削除、token復元のみ
   - workflow: credentials + token の両方をsecrets化

2. 二重投稿防止（冪等性）
   - publish_state.py: 日付ベースの投稿管理
   - automation_state.json: 投稿履歴を記録
   - 同日再実行時は自動スキップ

3. 部分成功対応
   - run_daily.py: try-except で各工程を分離
   - 記事成功/動画失敗でも記事は残る
   - 各段階でエラーログとexit code管理

4. 本番前安全設計
   - privacy_status='private' をデフォルト化
   - YouTube上限（タイトル100文字、説明5000文字）考慮
   - トークンリフレッシュ自動化

5. README表現修正
   - 「完成」→「初期実装完了」
   - 本番前確認事項を明記

これで本番運用の準備が整いました。

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix: sync portfolio stats and accuracy to agents table in ranking cron

The leaderboard was showing stale data (50% accuracy, +0.0% return for all
agents) because:
1. portfolio_return/portfolio_value were never synced from virtual_portfolios
2. time-decayed accuracy_score was computed but never written back

Now the ranking cron:

- Writes back time-decayed accuracy_score to agents table

Fix flavors API showing empty results after UTC midnight (#19)

hardcoding today's UTC date. This prevents the 7-hour window (midnight
UTC to 8am MT scrape) where the site showed all locations as empty.

commit.message:
Fix new job push: run push before in-app notifications + concurrent sends

Push notifications were never reached because the in-app notification
bulk INSERT (all drivers) ran first and likely caused function timeout.
Moved push block before in-app notifications and switched from sequential
await loop to Promise.allSettled for concurrent delivery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: VIES SOAP fault detection uses XML tag match instead of substring

The broad `includes('Fault')` check could false-positive on valid SOAP
responses where a company name contains the word "Fault". Now uses a
regex matching the actual XML `<Fault>` tag structure, consistent with
the data-bridge's direct VIES lookup.

commit.message:
fix(scoring): use actual bag penalty amount in round summary (#107)

bagPenalty was a boolean that only indicated whether a penalty occurred,
and the modal hardcoded "-100". This broke when config.bagPenalty != 100
(e.g. via mods) or when a team crossed the threshold multiple times in
one round. Changed bagPenalty to a number computed the same way as in
updateTeamScore (penaltyCount * config.bagPenalty).

commit.message:
feat(queue): implement server-side result queue for SQLite concurrency

Add ResultQueue system to handle concurrent writes from multiple analyzers:
- ResultQueue: in-memory queue with buffered channel (10000 items)
- BatchProcessor: single goroutine batch writer (50 items or 5s interval)
- ResultStorage: persistence layer for crash recovery (SQLite)
- Deduplication: 5-minute window to prevent duplicate processing

This solves SQLite concurrent write conflicts when multiple offline
analyzer workers submit results simultaneously.
